algorithms.title: Suspected Pediatric Ingestion/Exposure Field Approach
algorithms.description: Sequence for assessing and managing a suspected toxic ingestion or exposure.
steps: Ensure scene safety, especially for exposures involving chemicals or gases.
Identify the substance, amount, and time of exposure when possible; collect containers/pills/remnants to bring to the hospital.
Assess for toxidrome patterns (e.g., altered mental status + pinpoint pupils + respiratory depression suggesting opioid exposure) to guide urgency even without definitive identification.
If button battery ingestion is suspected: treat as a time-critical emergency regardless of current symptoms — rapid transport and hospital notification.
Support airway, breathing, and circulation as needed.
Transport promptly, bringing all identified substances/containers with the patient.

algorithms.title: Pediatric Submersion (Drowning) Field Management
algorithms.description: Sequence for managing a child after a submersion event.
steps: Ensure rescuer safety before entering the water/scene.
Remove the child from the water and begin airway/breathing support immediately — treat hypoxia as the core problem.
Apply spinal motion restriction only if the mechanism suggests trauma (e.g., diving injury).
Assess for hypothermia and begin gentle rewarming if present.
Transport for observation even if the child currently appears well, since pulmonary complications can evolve over hours.
Reassess respiratory status continuously during transport.
